records.ety: [L., fr. Gr. <?/, fr. <?/ to place, set. See Do, and cf. Anathema, Apothecary, Epithet, Hypothesis, Parenthesis, Theme, Tick a cover.]

defs: A position or proposition which a person advances and offers to maintain, or which is actually maintained by argument.
Hence, an essay or dissertation written upon specific or definite theme; especially, an essay presented by a candidate for a diploma or degree.
quotes: I told them of the grave, becoming, and sublime deportment they should assume upon this mystical occasion, and read them two homilies and a thesis of my own composing, to prepare them.

defs: An affirmation, or distinction from a supposition or hypothesis.
The accented part of the measure, expressed by the downward beat; -- the opposite of arsis.
The depression of the voice in pronouncing the syllables of a word.
The part of the foot upon which such a depression falls.
